content.problemStatement:
Vanja and Miksi really like games. After playing one game for a long time, they decided to invent another game!

In this game, they have a sequence A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \dots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​ and two numbers Z1Z_1Z1​ and Z2Z_2Z2​. The rules of the game are as follows:


The players take turns alternately, starting with Vanja.
There is an integer SSS; at the beginning, S=0S = 0S=0.
In each turn, the current player must choose an arbitrary element of AAA and either add that number to SSS or subtract it from SSS. Each element can be selected multiple times.
Afterwards, if S=Z1S = Z_1S=Z1​ or S=Z2S = Z_2S=Z2​, the current player (the player who made SSS equal to Z1Z_1Z1​ or Z2Z_2Z2​) is the winner of the game.
If the game lasts for 101010^{10}1010 turns, Vanja and Miksi decide to declare it a tie.
Can you help the boys determine the winner of the game? Please note that the game can end in a tie (if nobody can make S=Z1S = Z_1S=Z1​ or S=Z2S = Z_2S=Z2​ in the first 101010^{10}1010 moves).

Both players play optimally, i.e. if there is a move which guarantees the current player's victory regardless of the other player's moves, the current player will make such a move. If the current player cannot win and there is a move which guarantees that the game will end in a tie, the current player will make such a move.
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains three space-separated integers NNN, Z1Z_1Z1​ and Z2Z_2Z2​.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \dots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.
content.outputFormat:
For each test case, print a single line containing one integer — the final result of the game:

111 if Vanja (the first player) has a winning strategy
222 if Miksi (the second player) has a winning strategy
000 if the game ends in a tie

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: The first player can choose the value A1=−4A_1 = -4A1​=−4, subtract it from S=0S = 0S=0 and obtain S=−(−4)=4=Z2S = - (-4) = 4 = Z_2S=−(−4)=4=Z2​. The winner is the first player.
Example case 2: It can be proven that neither player is able to reach S=Z1S = Z_1S=Z1​ or S=Z2S = Z_2S=Z2​. The result is a tie.
